description:
# Extract a callable constant-folding primitive

## Goal

A pure function: given a `Prim` whose inputs are all literal Descrs,
return the literal Descr of its output (or `None` if not foldable).
This is the constant-fold core of `ir_fold`, exposed as a reusable
primitive the reducer will call from every clause-body it walks.

## What lands

A new module (or function added to an existing module) — likely
`crate::reducer::fold_prim(prim: &Prim, env: &HashMap<Var, Descr>) -> Option<Descr>`
or similar shape. Pure; no Module mutation.

Unit tests cover:
- `BinOp(Add, int_lit(3), int_lit(4))` → `int_lit(7)`
- Float arithmetic
- BinOp comparison folding (already in typer; mirror to reducer)
- `MakeTuple` of literal elems → literal tuple Descr
- `ListCons`/`MakeList` of literals → literal list Descr
- TupleField projection on a literal tuple → the element
- ListHead/ListTail on a literal list
- Unfoldable inputs (any var with non-literal Descr) → `None`

## Acceptance

- Unit tests pass.
- No production caller yet — primitive is dead code at this point,
  picked up by RED.3.

## Removals

None yet. `ir_fold` keeps its block-level driver. RED.8c may absorb
this work later.
title: fz-RED.1: pattern reduction primitive (pure fn)
